subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
Jamaica includes the following components:

- **1 game board** depicting the race course around Jamaica
- **6 ship holds** (one per player) with 5 cargo spaces each
- **6 pirate ships** in different colors
- **66 action cards** (11 per player, numbered 3–5 in two sections)
- **12 treasure cards** (with gold, cursed treasures, and special items)
- **45 gold tokens** (doubloons)
- **12 food tokens** and **12 gunpowder tokens**
- **2 dice** (one action die and one combat die)
- **9 treasure tokens**
- **1 compass token** (first player marker)

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
Set up the board as follows:

1. Place the **game board** in the center of the table.
2. Shuffle the **treasure cards** and place them face down on the marked space on the board.
3. Place **gold, food, and gunpowder tokens** in separate supply piles near the board.
4. Place **treasure tokens** on the designated port spaces around the board.
5. Give each player **3 doubloons** of gold to start.
6. Each player places their **pirate ship** on Port Royal (the starting space).

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
Each player receives:

1. A **ship hold board** with 5 cargo compartments. Each compartment can hold one type of cargo (gold, food, or gunpowder) or a treasure card.
2. A set of **11 action cards** in their chosen color. Shuffle these and deal **3 cards** to form your starting hand.
3. **3 gold doubloons** placed in one of your cargo holds.
4. Place your **ship miniature** on Port Royal.

The player who most recently visited an island (or the youngest player) takes the **compass token** and becomes the first captain.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
Jamaica is a **racing and resource management** game set in 1675 during the golden age of piracy. Players are pirate captains racing their ships around the island of Jamaica.

The goal is to complete the race around the island while collecting **gold and treasures** and engaging in pirate combat. The game lasts until one player crosses the finish line at Port Royal, after which the current round is completed.

Players score points based on:
- **Position on the track** (further ahead = more points, behind start = negative)
- **Gold doubloons** in cargo holds
- **Treasure cards** collected
- Minus points for **cursed treasures**

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each round follows these steps:

### 1. Roll the Dice
The **captain** (player with the compass) rolls both action dice. One die is assigned to the **morning action** and the other to the **evening action**. The captain chooses which die goes where.

### 2. Play Cards Simultaneously
All players **secretly select one action card** from their hand and reveal simultaneously. Each card has two sections:
- **Top section** → applies to the morning die
- **Bottom section** → applies to the evening die

The card section shows a symbol (move forward, move backward, load gold, load food, or load gunpowder). Multiply the die value by the card value for the result.

### 3. Resolve Actions (in player order)
Starting with the captain and going clockwise, each player resolves their **morning action** first, then their **evening action**:
- **Move forward/backward**: Move your ship that many spaces along the track.
- **Load cargo**: Take that many tokens of the indicated type and store them in your holds.

### 4. Pass the Compass
The compass passes **clockwise** to the next player, who becomes the new captain.

### 5. Draw Cards
Each player draws **one action card** from their personal draw pile.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
### Movement
- **Forward movement**: Move your ship forward along the numbered track spaces.
- **Backward movement**: Move your ship backward. This is sometimes useful to land on favorable port spaces.
- If you **land on or pass a port with a treasure token**, you may collect that treasure.

### Loading Cargo
- Cargo (gold, food, gunpowder) is stored in your **5 hold compartments**.
- Each compartment holds **one type** of cargo with no upper limit on tokens in that compartment.
- If all holds are full, you must **discard from one hold** entirely to make room.

### Combat
When your ship **lands on a space occupied by another player**, combat occurs:
1. The **attacker** rolls the combat die.
2. The **defender** rolls the combat die.
3. Both players may spend **gunpowder tokens** to add +1 per token to their roll.
4. **Higher total wins**. Ties go to the defender.
5. The **winner steals one cargo hold** from the loser — they take the entire contents of one compartment of their choice.

### Treasure Cards
When you collect a treasure card, look at it secretly and store it face down in a hold:
- **Gold treasures** are worth bonus points.
- **Special items** grant unique abilities (e.g., extra combat power).
- **Cursed treasures** are worth **negative points** and cannot be voluntarily discarded.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
### Port Spaces
Some spaces on the track are **ports** with special effects:
- **Treasure ports**: Collect a treasure card when landing here.
- **Tax ports**: You must pay food tokens equal to the port's cost. If you cannot pay, you move backward to the previous port.

### Food Costs
Several spaces require **food payment** to dock. If you lack food, your ship is sent backward to the nearest free port that doesn't require food.

### Full Holds
You only have **5 cargo compartments**. Managing your limited space is critical. You must sometimes sacrifice one type of cargo to make room for another.

### Pirate Lair
If you land exactly on the **Pirate Lair** space, you gain a special combat advantage for the remainder of the game.

### Crossing the Finish Line
Once any player **reaches or passes Port Royal** (the finish), the current round is completed so all players take their actions. Then the game ends immediately.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
The game ends when a player **crosses the finish line at Port Royal** and the current round is completed.

### Final Scoring
1. **Position on the track**: Each space on the board has a point value printed on it. Players earn (or lose) points based on their final position. Spaces behind Port Royal have **negative values**.
2. **Gold doubloons**: Each gold token in your holds is worth **1 point**.
3. **Treasure cards**: Reveal all treasure cards and add their point values (positive or negative).
4. **Cursed treasures**: Subtract points for any cursed treasure cards.

### Winning
The player with the **highest total score** wins. In case of a tie, the player furthest along the track wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Balance speed and cargo**: Moving fast is important, but gold and treasures are what win the game.
- **Keep some gunpowder**: Having 2–3 gunpowder tokens deters attacks and wins combats.
- **Food is essential**: Running out of food at a tax port sends you backward. Always keep a small food reserve.
- **Manage your holds carefully**: With only 5 compartments, plan ahead for what cargo you need.
- **Don't ignore treasure ports**: Treasure cards can be worth significant points.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Aggressive pirate**: Stock up on gunpowder and intentionally land on opponents to steal their cargo. Target players with gold-heavy holds.
- **Speed racer**: Focus on forward movement to claim the best position bonus. The track position points can be very significant.
- **Treasure hunter**: Prioritize landing on treasure ports. Some treasures are worth 3–5 points each, rivaling large gold stockpiles.
- **Captain advantage**: When you're the captain, assign dice strategically. Put the higher die on the action that benefits you most and hurts opponents.
- **Backward movement**: Sometimes moving backward deliberately lets you land on a treasure port or avoid a costly tax port.
- **Late-game push**: Save high-movement cards for the final rounds to surge ahead on the track for maximum position points.
